hero.headline: Connecting Young Hands to the Future
hero.subtitle: Fun Connect provides IoT learning kits that turn abstract technology into tangible creations — from snap-together assembly to drag-and-drop coding. Perfect for classrooms, clubs, and parent-child learning.

features.title: Why Fun Connect?
features.subtitle: We don't just sell kits — we design complete classroom experiences
features.f1d: Every sensor and actuator is an independent module with magnetic snap connectors. Even young learners can start building in minutes.
features.f2d: 12 progressive lessons covering basic circuits to smart home projects, with teacher guides and worksheets included.
features.f3d: Drag-and-drop blocks to build logic. Supports both Scratch and Python, bridging seamlessly to advanced development.
features.f4d: Built-in Wi-Fi module lets creations connect to the cloud — monitor sensor data in real time from a phone dashboard.
features.f5d: Dedicated expansion packs for science fairs and robotics competitions, plus one-on-one technical advisor consultations.
features.f6d: Full series certified by Taiwan BSMI and EU CE standards. Low-voltage design with rounded edges for complete peace of mind.

journey.title: From Classroom to Real Life: The Learning Journey
journey.subtitle: Four stages of guided learning, from absolute beginner to independent creator
journey.s1d: Assemble sensor modules and understand basic circuits
journey.s2d: Control lights and motors with block-based language
journey.s3d: Stream real-time data to your phone via Wi-Fi
journey.s4d: Design your own smart device and share it on stage

testimonials.title: What Teachers Say
testimonials.t1q: Fun Connect's modules let my students build an automatic watering system in just two class periods. The moment their eyes lit up, I knew this kit was the right choice.
testimonials.t1r: Minsheng Elementary School, Taipei · IT Teacher
testimonials.t2q: The lesson plans are incredibly thoughtful — even a non-technical teacher like me can lead the class. The visual interface truly has zero barrier to entry; third graders can jump right in.
testimonials.t2r: Dadun Junior High School, Taichung · Science Teacher
testimonials.t3q: We won the city-wide science fair with Fun Connect's expansion pack. The technical advisor replied to my messages on weekends — this level of support is really rare.
testimonials.t3r: Yangming Elementary School, Kaohsiung · Science Fair Advisor

cta.title: Ready to Transform Your Classroom?
cta.subtitle: Request a trial kit and experience the magic of Fun Connect firsthand

product.title: From Hands-On Assembly to Cloud Connection
product.subtitle: Three core kits, each designed for a different learning stage and theme

basic.name: Basic Sensor Kit
basic.desc: 6 sensors + 3 actuators, ideal for upper elementary students new to IoT. From lighting up the first LED to reading temperature and humidity — see results in a single class period.

smart.name: Smart Home Kit
smart.desc: From automated lighting to burglar alarms, real-world scenarios drive learning motivation. Built-in Wi-Fi cloud module lets students monitor sensor data from their phones — this is real IoT.

comp.name: Competition Expansion Pack
comp.desc: High-precision sensors + aluminum structural parts — the ultimate toolkit for science fairs and robotics competitions. Includes 3 one-on-one technical advisor sessions from planning to presentation.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: Absolutely none! The Basic Kit uses Scratch-based block language — just drag, drop, and combine blocks. Step-by-step video tutorials are included.
faq.q2: How many kits does a class need?
faq.a2: We recommend one kit per 2–3 students. For a typical 30-student class, about 10–12 kits. Bulk discounts available for orders of 10+.
faq.a3: Of course! All modules come with a one-year warranty. Out-of-warranty parts are eligible for discounted replacement. Spare parts also available individually.
faq.a4: Yes! We hold one-day teacher workshops in different cities each semester, covering hardware operation, lesson plan walkthroughs, and classroom management tips. Free of charge with PD hours included.

cases.title: Real Stories from the Classroom
cases.subtitle: Trusted by over 120 schools — see how teachers and students create magic with Fun Connect
cases.c1d: Fifth graders used the Basic Sensor Kit to build a greenhouse monitoring system that detects soil moisture and auto-waters. Won an excellence award at the Tainan Science Fair.
cases.c2d: Eighth-grade IT club used the Smart Home Kit to auto-control fans and lights — saving 15% on electricity in one semester.
cases.c3d: A high school team equipped a DIY drone with the Competition Pack's barometer and GPS to monitor campus air quality, advancing to the national finals.
cases.c4d: Rural elementary students built a landslide warning device combining a rain sensor with LINE notifications, earning Ministry of Education recognition.
cases.c5d: Marine education meets IoT — students created a drifting buoy tracking ocean plastic via GPS, winning the Most Popular Award at an inter-school symposium.
cases.c6d: 48 schools and 200+ teachers and students gathered to showcase smart devices built with Fun Connect. Live voting selected the most popular project.

about.title: Lighting Up Every Classroom with Technology
about.subtitle: A team of edtech believers who know that 'learning by doing' transforms education
about.mission1: Fun Connect was founded in 2019. We noticed that STEM teaching tools were either too expensive or too complex. Teachers wanted to bring IoT into their classrooms but hit the hardware barrier. So we built our own: modular, safe, affordable, with complete lesson plans.
about.mission2: Five years later, we've reached over 120 schools across Taiwan — from Taipei to Hualien, from cities to indigenous communities. Every child deserves to feel the power of technology in their own hands.
about.p1r:
Founder & CEO
Former IT Teacher · M.Ed. in EdTech
about.p3r:
Chief Education Officer
Curriculum Design · 15 Years Teaching
about.p4r:
Product Design
Industrial Design Background · LEGO Enthusiast
about.t1d: Three co-founders sketched the first circuit diagram at a café near Taipei Main Station.
about.t2d: The first 500 units sold out in two weeks, with trial requests from 27 schools.
about.t3d: Listed in the 108 Curriculum technology domain procurement catalog. Partner schools surpassed 60.
about.t4d: Added Wi-Fi cloud module. Won the Taiwan Excellence Award the same year.
about.t5d: High-precision expansion designed for science fairs and robotics competitions. Technical advisory service launched simultaneously.
about.t6d: Hosted the inaugural Maker Carnival with 48 schools and hundreds of participants — a landmark annual edtech event.
